tasks.spec: Act as the first independent, read-only admission judge for Bead hermes-jxk0. Do not mutate Paperclip, Beads, repositories, services, or configuration. Query the live local Paperclip API at http://127.0.0.1:3100/api for company 87c32b8e-f131-4df8-ad8e-963d01b458e7. Inspect current issues and enough agent/run state to decide whether ten DISTINCT agent-safe Paperclip issues can be executed consecutively now. Exclude personal/human-owned work, blocked work, already-running work, synthetic Ringer-only repetitions, duplicates, and any issue lacking a safe autonomous acceptance path. Never print or store credentials. Write exactly one artifact, queue-admission.json, as valid JSON with these top-level keys: generated_at_utc, source, bead_id, constraints, eligible_candidates, exclusions, eligible_count, verdict, next_action. source must identify the live Paperclip API without query strings or secrets. bead_id must equal hermes-jxk0. constraints must be a non-empty array. eligible_candidates must be an array of objects; each object must include id, identifier, title, status, assigneeAgentId, and rationale. exclusions must be a non-empty array of objects containing identifier and reason. eligible_count must exactly equal the length of eligible_candidates. verdict must be exactly ADMIT or HOLD; ADMIT is permitted only if eligible_count is at least 10 and all ten are distinct, currently actionable, non-personal, and not running. next_action must be a concrete safe action. Be conservative and factual. Do not invent candidates or claim execution occurred.
tasks.verified: The live Paperclip queue was independently screened against the ten-distinct-task safety and actionability contract.

tasks.spec: Act as the second independent, read-only gate judge for Bead hermes-jxk0. Do not mutate Paperclip, Beads, repositories, services, or configuration. Query live Paperclip state at http://127.0.0.1:3100/api for company 87c32b8e-f131-4df8-ad8e-963d01b458e7. Write exactly one artifact named streak-gate.md. It must define an evidence-backed admission decision for a future streak of ten DISTINCT real agent-safe Paperclip issues. It must include the literal headings: # Verdict, ## Live evidence, ## Eligibility rules, ## Sequential execution protocol, ## Reset conditions, ## Receipt schema, ## Current candidate assessment, ## Safe next action. Under # Verdict, state exactly either ADMIT or HOLD on its own line. ADMIT is allowed only if the live queue contains at least ten distinct, currently actionable, non-personal, non-running issues with safe autonomous completion paths. The protocol must require one-at-a-time execution, pre/post live read-back, successful terminal run state, done issue state, zero adapter/runtime errors, immutable evidence per issue, and a full reset to zero after any error. The receipt schema must name issue UUID, identifier, agent UUID, run UUID, start/end timestamps, terminal state, exit code, error reason, evidence path, and sequence number. Explicitly exclude personal/human-owned issues, blocked issues, already-running issues, synthetic repetitions, and duplicate IDs. Never include credential values. Do not claim ten tasks were executed; this is an admission gate only.
tasks.check:
set -eu; f=streak-gate.md; if [ ! -s "$f" ]; then echo 'FAIL: streak-gate.md missing or empty' >&2; exit 1; fi; for heading in '# Verdict' '## Live evidence' '## Eligibility rules' '## Sequential execution protocol' '## Reset conditions' '## Receipt schema' '## Current candidate assessment' '## Safe next action'; do if ! grep -Fxq -- "$heading" "$f"; then printf 'FAIL: missing exact heading: %s
' "$heading" >&2; exit 1; fi; done; for term in 'one-at-a-time' 'pre/post live read-back' 'done issue state' 'adapter/runtime errors' 'reset to zero' 'issue UUID' 'run UUID' 'sequence number' 'personal/human-owned' 'blocked issues' 'already-running' 'synthetic repetitions' 'duplicate IDs'; do if ! grep -Fiq -- "$term" "$f"; then printf 'FAIL: missing required contract phrase: %s
' "$term" >&2; exit 1; fi; done; verdict=$(awk '/^# Verdict$/{getline; while ($0 ~ /^[[:space:]]*$/) getline; print; exit}' "$f"); case "$verdict" in ADMIT|HOLD) ;; *) printf 'FAIL: verdict must be ADMIT or HOLD, found: %s
' "$verdict" >&2; exit 1;; esac; bytes=$(wc -c < "$f" | tr -d ' '); if [ "$bytes" -lt 3000 ]; then printf 'FAIL: streak gate too small: %s bytes
' "$bytes" >&2; exit 1; fi; printf 'EXECUTED_CHECK=PASS protocol-gate verdict=%s bytes=%s
' "$verdict" "$bytes"
tasks.verified: A second independent judge defined and applied the strict sequential streak protocol, reset conditions, and immutable receipt contract.
